input:
For another, Abū Lahab might have been tempted to create trouble if he was left out. More importantly, however, the Prophet never despaired of a change of heart taking place, even in the case of a determined enemy. So Abū Lahab was among the Prophet’s guests. This time, however, the Prophet took the initiative and spoke to his relatives immediately after they finished eating. He said: Praise be to God! I praise Him, seek His support, believe in Him and rely on Him.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 7: The Call to Islam Goes Public

output.scene_title: The Prophet's Mercy Towards Abū Lahab
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 7: The Call to Islam Goes Public

characters.name: Abū Lahab ibn Abd al-Muṭṭalib
characters.appearance: Late 40s to early 50s, dark brown to black skin, strong build, thick beard with a few grey streaks, neatly kept. Wearing an expensive-looking dark brown thobe with intricate patterns, a luxurious black cloak draped over shoulders, a red head cloth wrapped around his head. Worn leather sandals. Brooding expression, stern eyes.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a beige cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by beige cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Other relatives and guests
characters.appearance: Varied ages, skin tones, and builds. Dressed in simple clothing, with some wearing head coverings. Gathered around a low table, hands visible, faces not visible.

output.moral_lesson: The Prophet's mercy and compassion extended to all people, even those who opposed him.
output.narrative_text: The Prophet invited his relatives, including his uncle Abū Lahab, to a meal, showing that he was willing to reach out to those who opposed him. After they finished eating, the Prophet gave thanks to God and praised Him.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, backlit by doorway light. Abū Lahab shown with full face, expression stern. Other guests shown as silhouettes, faces not visible. Warm emotional scene, appropriate for children.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Interior of a modest 7th century Makkah mud-brick home. A dark-skinned man in a luxurious black cloak, red head cloth, thick beard, sitting on a low bench looking annoyed, arms crossed. In the background, a slender older Arab man in white thobe and beige cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing at an open doorway with warm golden afternoon sunlight streaming behind him. Inside, a few relatives and guests dressed in simple clothing, sitting on a woven mat with their hands visible, faces not visible. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.

metadata.chapter_title: The Call to Islam Goes Public
